title: Longest Consecutive Numbers in an Array

content.problemStatement:
You are given an array numsnumsnums containing integers.

Your task is to find the **size of the largest subset of consecutive integers**.
The order of elements in the original array does not matter ŌĆö they can be rearranged to form the consecutive sequence.
The test cases may contain duplicate values.

Function DeclarationFunction NamelongestConsecutivelongestConsecutivelongestConsecutive ŌĆō This function returns the length of the longest sequence of consecutive integers in the array.

Parameters
numsnumsnums : An array of integers (may contain duplicates and negative values).
Return Value
Returns a single integer ŌĆö the **length of the longest consecutive integer sequence**.
Constraints
1Ōēżnums.lengthŌēż1051 \leq nums.length \leq 10^51Ōēżnums.lengthŌēż105
ŌłÆ109Ōēżnums[i]Ōēż109ŌłÆ10^9 \leq nums[i] \leq 10^9ŌłÆ109Ōēżnums[i]Ōēż109
Duplicate values may exist

Input Format


The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.




Each test case consists of multiple lines of input:



The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ŌĆö the number of elements in the array.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers nums[i]nums[i]nums[i] ŌĆö the elements of the array.




Output Format

For each test case, print a single integer ŌĆö the size of the longest consecutive sequence.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the first test case the longest run of consecutive numbers is [12, 13, 14, 15, 16], which has length 5.
For the second test case the longest consecutive sequence is [1, 2, 3, 4], giving us length 4.
For the third test case the longest consecutive group is [100, 101, 102, 103].
